payload.title: Consulting work stays off the public record — metrics can stay, attribution goes
payload.body:
Decided that Avatour and other consulting engagements will not appear on the Track Record page. I have not broadcast the layoff or the consulting work publicly. When I return to Wells within 30 days, my HR record is being resurrected — I am not coming in as new. The public narrative will be that I moved teams.

The consulting work is real and valuable — VP-level product strategy, 3.2x TAM expansion, AI-first pivot. But surfacing it publicly creates a Wells to Consulting to Wells timeline that reads as regression to anyone scanning a career page.

The four-zone structure makes this easy. Metrics from consulting can be included as capability evidence without naming Avatour. In 1:1 conversations I can mention startup experience as additional context. But the page should not create a timeline that invites scrutiny.

This feels like the right call but there is tension — the startup work is exactly the kind of experience that would counter the banking bias if it were visible. The tradeoff is that making it visible creates a worse problem (the regression narrative) than the one it solves.
payload.decision: Consulting metrics stay, company attribution goes.
payload.alternatives_considered: Showing Avatour prominently as proof of startup experience.
payload.open_questions: How to handle in resume customization for roles where startup experience is weighted heavily.
